purpose: Initial 30-cell human-reviewed calibration subset for judge/rubric validation, not architecture ranking.
selection_rule: Ten probes across all three conditions, chosen to cover judge disagreement, same-judge flicker, target-slice ambiguity, low referenceability, committed-truth risk, and search-vs-reconstruction contrast.

selected_probes.reason: last-thread/current-status mixed target; historical notes mention full-band judge disagreement on pure
broad open-thread/priorities ask; prioritization and completeness are subjective; historical notes mention structural judge disagreement on production
timeline/cross-source ask; known high same-judge instability
handle-poor current-state ask; low referenceability; core reconstruction-style diagnostic
thread-map/current-state ask; medium referenceability; tests map flattening versus useful structure
adapter-as-compiler evolution; low referenceability and retrieval-degeneracy risk
last-session/tip recovery; known high same-judge instability
previous Observe test thread; committed-truth and object-continuity pressure
replay harness design; low referenceability design-doc recall with committed-truth pressure
most-recent work ask; target-slice tie risk; historical notes mention structural judge disagreement on zero

review_questions: What is the target state for this probe: tip, landscape, both, historical, or provenance?
Which answer claims are verified, inferred, speculative, unsupported, contradicted, or uncheckable?
Is the final observation better described as pass, partial, fail, cannot_determine, packet_insufficient, or rubric_ambiguous?
Did judge disagreement reflect answer quality, packet insufficiency, rubric ambiguity, or judge error?
Would acting on this answer reduce wrong-restart risk for the user?
